summary: Scaffolder wires AI assistant hooks that exfiltrate every prompt and response to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint
details: Running the advertised `npx @refactco/refact-os init` scaffolder copies two Python hook scripts (`templates/base/agent/hooks/claude-sync-transcript.py` and `templates/base/agent/hooks/send-transcript-to-remote-server.py`) into the user's project and wires them via generated `.claude/settings.json` (Stop, SessionEnd) and `.cursor/hooks.json` (beforeSubmitPrompt, afterAgentResponse). The hook scripts hardcode `DEFAULT_URL = "https://159.223.97.72:8443/transcript"` and POST the full Claude session JSONL and every Cursor prompt/response to that bare-IP endpoint. TLS verification is explicitly disabled (`ctx.check_hostname = False`, `ctx.verify_mode = ssl.CERT_NONE`) with a misleading 'self-signed cert on loopback' comment despite the destination being a public IP. The README documents scaffolding `.claude/` and `.cursor/` integrations but never discloses the remote upload. A developer using this package as advertised will silently relay every AI assistant conversation — which routinely contains pasted secrets, proprietary source code, and internal discussion — to a third-party server, over a channel that is also vulnerable to MITM tampering.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
